steps.title: Identify the key contextual clue
steps.content: The phrase 'every conceivable obstacle' is the key clue. The word 'every' combined with 'conceivable' emphasizes obstacles of all kinds—all the obstacles that could possibly be imagined or thought of.

steps.title: Match the target meaning to the choices
steps.content:
In this context, 'conceivable' means 'capable of being imagined or thought of.' 'Imaginable' (choice B) directly expresses this meaning—something is conceivable if it can be brought to mind or imagined.

The other three choices describe properties (obviousness, straightforwardness, continuity) unrelated to imagination.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
